macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Global Tech Selloff Drives Major Index Declines as AI Rally Faces Reassessment
items.summary: Asian markets slid sharply with Japan's Nikkei falling 4.34% and Taiwan dropping up to 6% amid a global rout in technology stocks, while U.S. stock index futures slid 0.4% and the Nasdaq fell 0.6% as investors reassess the AI-fueled rally following weak Netflix forecasts and deepening chip stock selloffs.

items.title: Fed Rate Hike Odds Rise as Oil Surges on US-Iran Conflict and Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Chances of a Federal Reserve rate hike in July increased as Brent crude nears $85 a barrel and oil hits a one-month high due to renewed US-Iran hostilities threatening the Strait of Hormuz, while softer-than-expected June inflation data initially led traders to cut bets on imminent hikes before oil prices reversed sentiment.

items.title: Netflix Tumbles 9% on Weak Earnings Forecast; Oracle and Samsung Lead Tech Declines
items.summary: Netflix shares fell 9% after issuing a weak earnings forecast that deepened doubts over growth, while Oracle Corporation dropped 6.25% and Samsung Electronics fell 8.77%, contributing to the broader technology sector selloff ahead of key Alphabet and Intel earnings reports expected to sway the AI trade.

items.title: US Imposes New 25% Tariffs on Brazilian Imports Amid Trade Tensions
items.summary: The United States announced new 25% tariffs on certain Brazilian imports over alleged unfair practices, though beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials were exempted, adding to global trade uncertainty as oil prices remain volatile due to Gulf refining disruptions.

items.title: Dollar Set for Weekly Decline While ECB Holds Rates Amid Euro Zone Surplus Widening
items.summary: The dollar is poised for a weekly decline as traders reduce bets on Fed rate hikes following softer inflation data, while the European Central Bank is expected to hold rates after a recent hike, coinciding with data showing the euro zone's current account surplus widened in May.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Citadel Securities Invests $400M in Crypto.com at $20B Valuation
items.summary: Citadel Securities made a major $400 million investment in Crypto.com, valuing the exchange at $20 billion, marking a significant deal in the crypto ecosystem.

items.title: Bitcoin Slides Below $64K Amid Iran Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin fell below $64,000 to trade around $63,457–$63,600 as fresh U.S. airstrikes on Iran increased geopolitical uncertainty and weakened risk sentiment.

items.title: Ethereum Reclaims $1,820 After Nine Months of Lower Lows
items.summary: Ethereum rebounded to $1,820, supported by $53.9 million net inflows into U.S. spot ETH ETFs, reigniting debate over whether a market bottom is forming.

items.title: T. Rowe Price Launches First Actively Managed Multi-Token Crypto ETF
items.summary: $1.9 trillion asset manager T. Rowe Price launched its first actively managed multi-token crypto ETF, expanding institutional access to diversified crypto exposure.

items.title: Visa Launches Internal Stablecoin Platform Offering Access to OUSD
items.summary: Visa is launching an internal stablecoin platform for clients that provides access to Open USD (OUSD), positioning itself as a rival to Circle in the stablecoin space.

items.title: BitPay Receives MiCA License for EU-Wide Crypto and Stablecoin Services
items.summary: BitPay secured a MiCA license from Dutch regulators, unlocking crypto and stablecoin payment services across the entire European Union.

items.title: Taiwan Sentences BitShine Ringleader to 22 Years in $39M Fraud Case
items.summary: Taiwan courts sentenced the leader of the BitShine crypto exchange to 22 years in prison for orchestrating a $39 million fraud scheme.

items.title: NovaBox Loses 99.86% of ETH Reward Pool in Flash Loan Exploit
items.summary: A flash loan attack drained nearly all funds from NovaBox’s Ethereum reward pool, causing losses of approximately 56.73 ETH.

items.title: New York Proposes Stablecoin Rules Aligning with GENIUS Act
items.summary: New York’s DFS proposed new regulations to update and strengthen its stablecoin framework, aiming to align with the federal GENIUS Act.

items.title: Ondo Surges 3.17% Following DTCC Tokenized Stocks Launch
items.summary: Ondo (ONDO) rose 3.17% in the last 6 hours due to continued buying momentum after launching DTCC-backed tokenized stocks.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi K3: World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model at 2.8 Trillion Parameters
items.summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI released Kimi K3 on July 16, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open MoE model claiming performance approaching Anthropic's frontier Fable model and rivaling OpenAI, marking China's largest open-weight AI system.

items.title: Thinking Machines Lab Releases Inkling: Mira Murati's First Open Model to Challenge AI Giants
items.summary: CEO Mira Murati's startup launched Inkling, a 975B-parameter mixture-of-experts model with 41B active parameters and a 1M-token context window, designed to offer customizable AI as an alternative to one-size-fits-frontier models from OpenAI and Anthropic.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-Red: Automated Red-Teaming Model Cuts Prompt Injection Failures by 6x
items.summary: OpenAI unveiled GPT-Red, an internal automated red-teaming LLM trained via self-play reinforcement learning that reduced GPT-5.6 Sol's prompt injection failures by 6x and achieved 84% attack success against defender models versus 13% for human red-teamers.

items.title: Nvidia Announces Cosmos 3 Edge and Expands Physical AI Ecosystem in Japan with Fanuc and Yaskawa
items.summary: Nvidia launched its new AI model Cosmos 3 Edge and announced partnerships with Japanese robotics firms Fanuc and Yaskawa Electric to advance robotics and physical AI development in Japan.

items.title: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs Publish Joint Bioresilience Strategy for Biological Threats
items.summary: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs released a comprehensive bioresilience strategy covering prevention, detection, and response to biological threats, aiming to curb AI misuse in biology while aiding outbreak response.

items.title: Apple Accuses OpenAI of Stealing Trade Secrets While Securing China Approval for AI Tools
items.summary: Apple accused OpenAI of stealing trade secrets to build its own ChatGPT hardware, while simultaneously securing Chinese government approval for its AI system, clearing a major roadblock for iPhone sales in China.

items.title: Trump Administration Lifts Anthropic Claude Ban; Meta Pulls Instagram AI Feature Amid Criticism
items.summary: The Trump administration ended a weekslong ban on Anthropic's latest Claude chatbot versions tied to cybersecurity concerns, while Meta disabled a recently launched AI tool feature after criticism it enabled Instagram accounts to be used for AI-generated images.

items.title: Databricks Valued at $188 Billion Following $3 Billion Coatue Investment Led Round
items.summary: Investment firm Coatue Management is leading a $3 billion investment in data analytics provider Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion, according to Wall Street Journal reports citing people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Google Gemini 3.5 Pro Release Delayed Months as Alphabet Struggles with Coding Capabilities
items.summary: Alphabet's Google is months behind schedule on releasing Gemini 3.5 Pro, its most powerful flagship AI model, as the company works to improve capabilities particularly in coding, according to Bloomberg News.

items.title: Neko Health Raises $700 Million to Expand AI Body Scans in US Starting with New York Clinic
items.summary: AI health startup Neko Health secured $700 million to expand its AI body scan technology in the United States, with plans to open its first US clinic in New York.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Roblox Launches Mobile AI Game Creation Tool 'Build' on July 16
items.summary: Roblox announced its new mobile-first AI creation tool, 'Build,' allowing users to create games directly on their phones, expanding the platform beyond play and publishing.

items.title: Marvel's Wolverine Release Date Confirmed for September 15, 2026
items.summary: Insomniac Games showcased a new cinematic trailer titled 'Ain't No Hero' for Marvel's Wolverine, reaffirming the official launch date of September 15, 2026.

items.title: Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival Confirms Doug Bradley as Pinhead Voice
items.summary: A new developer diary for Hellraiser: Revival confirms series veteran Doug Bradley returns as the voice of Pinhead, with the game set for October 8, 2026 on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.

items.title: NHL 27 Trailer Reveals Online Mode Return and Arena Overhauls
items.summary: EA Sports released a reveal trailer for NHL 27, highlighting the return of a missing online mode and overhauls to all 32 arenas, with a launch date of September 4, 2026.

items.title: EA Sports FC 27 Slated for September 25, 2026 Launch Across All Platforms
items.summary: Insider billbil-kun reports that EA Sports FC 27 will launch on September 25, 2026, supporting PS4, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch, PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and the new Switch 2.

items.title: Mortal Shell II PS5 Physical Edition Sells Out Due to High Demand
items.summary: Mortal Shell II's PS5-exclusive physical disc edition has gone out of stock, signaling strong consumer demand for physical game media despite industry shifts toward digital.

items.title: Grand Theft Auto VI Pre-Orders Generate $260 Million in First Week
items.summary: A new report indicates GTA VI generated $260 million in pre-orders during its first week, with projections suggesting the game could hit $5 billion at launch.

items.title: Netflix Confirms 300 Titles Have Used Generative AI
items.summary: Netflix revealed that approximately 300 of its titles have utilized generative AI, marking a significant adoption of AI technology in content production.

items.title: PlayStation Faces Antitrust Complaint in Mexico Over Physical Discs
items.summary: Sony is facing an antitrust complaint in Mexico regarding its plans to phase out physical game discs, sparking regulatory scrutiny over the digital shift.

items.title: The Duskbloods Network Test Scheduled for August with Signups Starting Next Week
items.summary: The Duskbloods has announced a closed network test for August, with player signups beginning next week for the upcoming stealth-horror title.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft for AI Gadgets
items.summary: Apple filed a bombshell lawsuit on Friday accusing OpenAI and two former employees of stealing trade secrets to develop the iPhone maker's yet-to-be-unveiled AI hardware, sending shockwaves through Silicon Valley.

items.title: Stripe and Advent Make $53B Offer to Acquire PayPal
items.summary: Stripe and private equity firm Advent International submitted a joint acquisition offer for PayPal Holdings at $60.50 per share, valuing the payments company at over $53 billion and causing PayPal shares to soar 17%.

items.title: SpaceX Starship Launch Aborted on Pad at Last Moment
items.summary: SpaceX's latest Starship launch was aborted just moments before liftoff at the launch pad, while the company's shares recently dropped below its $135 IPO price as its market rally fizzles.

items.title: Amazon to Launch Satellite Internet in South Africa
items.summary: Amazon is set to launch its satellite internet service in South Africa, appearing to beat Elon Musk's Starlink to the market in Musk's homeland.

items.title: TSMC Pledges Another $100 Billion for US Chipmaking Expansion
items.summary: Taiwan semiconductor giant TSMC announced plans to invest an additional $100 billion to expand its manufacturing capacity in the United States.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large AI Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York state will block the construction of new large data centers that fuel artificial intelligence for up to a year to protect the environment and the energy grid, a move President Trump has criticized.

items.title: Chinese Blogger Sentenced to 20 Months for Falsely Claiming Xiaomi SU7 Unsafe
items.summary: A Chinese blogger was imprisoned for 20 months for fabricating negative safety claims about Xiaomi's SU7 electric sedan, according to state media reports on Friday.

items.title: South Korea Fines Coupang Over Data Leak, Drawing US Criticism
items.summary: South Korea's decision to fine e-commerce firm Coupang over a data leak has drawn criticism from Washington and raised concerns about the country's openness to American tech companies.

items.title: Meta Reins in AI Tool After Criticism Over Instagram Image Access
items.summary: Meta pulled a feature of a recently launched AI tool following criticism that it automatically accessed public Instagram images to create AI-generated content without user consent.

items.title: EU Issues New Rules Requiring Google to Share Search Data and Open Android
items.summary: The European Union issued new regulations requiring Google to share search data and open its Android system to rival companies, intensifying regulatory pressure on the tech giant.
